citations.note: Preamble: Recipients may use this work commercially, use technical protection measures, combine this data or database with other databases or data, and share their changes and additions.
§3.3(a) fallback licence: These rights explicitly include commercial use, and do not exclude any field of endeavour.
Preamble: this document is intended to allow you to freely share, modify, and use this work for any purpose.
Definition of "Use": includes modifying the Database or Data.
§3.1: The Rightsholder dedicates the Work to the public domain and relinquishes all rights in Copyright and Database Rights.
§3.1 Dedication: the Rightsholder dedicates the Work to the public domain for the benefit of the public and relinquishes all rights in Copyright and Database Rights.
§3.1: The above relinquishment of rights applies worldwide and includes media and formats now known or created in the future.
Preamble: Because this document places the database and its contents in or as close as possible within the public domain, there are no restrictions or requirements placed upon recipients.
§4.2 Relationship to patents: This Document does not grant You a licence for any patents that the Rightsholder may own.

permissions.commentary: Like CC0, PDDL carves patents out of the dedication entirely. §4.2 makes this explicit: no patent licence is granted. The license is silent on patent use (neither permitted nor forbidden); any patents the Rightsholder holds are preserved, and users are cautioned to clear patent rights separately. This is the data-license analog of the CC0 §4(a) patent non-grant that caused OSI to reject CC0 for software.

citations.note: Preamble: It is not a requirement that recipients provide further users with a copy of this licence or attribute the original creator of the data or database as a source.

conditions.commentary: PDDL imposes no notice-retention or license-inclusion obligation: the preamble explicitly disclaims any requirement to pass on a copy of the licence. The Rightsholder has surrendered the relevant rights rather than licensing them conditionally.

PDDL is a public-domain dedication. Downstream users may relicense any Derivative Database under any terms, including proprietary, because there are no rights left with the Rightsholder to condition on.

citations.note: Preamble: It is not a requirement that recipients provide further users with a copy of this licence or attribute the original creator of the data or database as a source.

conditions.commentary: Attribution is not a licensing condition under PDDL. Open Data Commons publishes separate Community Norms that recommend credit as a matter of scholarly practice, but §4.1 expressly states Community Norms are not a contract and do not form part of the Document.

citations.note: §5.2: Subject to any liability that may not be excluded or limited by law, the Rightsholder is not liable for, and expressly excludes, all liability for loss or damage.
§5.2: This exclusion of liability includes, but is not limited to, any special, incidental, consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages.
§5.1: The Work is provided by the Rightsholder as is and without any warranty of any kind, either express or implied, whether of title, of accuracy or completeness.
§4.3 Relationship to trade marks: This Document does not grant You a licence for any trade marks that the Rightsholder may own or that the Rightsholder may use to cover the Work.

limitations.commentary: Parallel to the patent carve-out: §4.3 confirms PDDL does not grant a trademark licence. Default trademark law applies unmodified; the Rightsholder retains all trademark rights.

sources.source: Open Data Commons PDDL v1.0 text
